commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a35b70d6c3673238244d908f8afd9227af4037bd)
commands.review_summary:
The branch forwards config into the legacy core browser tool factory, applies image sanitization to legacy browser screenshot results, and adds legacy browser-tool tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Current-main source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: the documented config exists, while the active browser:screenshot result path calls imageResultFromFile without imageSanitization.
commands.review_followup: Human handling is needed because this external PR has mock-only proof, is conflicting, and must be ported from deleted legacy files to the active browser plugin before merge.

target.title: fix(agents): pass imageMaxDimensionPx config to browser:screenshot
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

  - Problem: The browser:screenshot tool ignores the agents.defaults.imageMaxDimensionPx config, always resizing images to 1200px regardless of user settings.                                                                             
  - Why it matters: Users cannot control screenshot image quality/dimensions; large screenshots are unnecessarily compressed.
  - What changed: Added config option to createBrowserTool() and passed imageSanitization to screenshot results.                                                                                                                           
  - What did NOT change: Other browser tool actions remain unaffected; default behavior (1200px) unchanged when no config set.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
  - Users can now set agents.defaults.imageMaxDimensionPx to control browser screenshot resize limits.

## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? (No)
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
  - New/changed network calls? (No)
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
  - Data access scope changed? (No)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

  - OS: Any
  - Runtime/container: OpenClaw gateway
  - Model/provider: Any
  - Integration/channel (if any): browser tool

### Steps
  1. Set config agents.defaults.imageMaxDimensionPx: 2000
  2. Run browser tool with action: "screenshot"
  3. Check logs for maxDimensionPx value

### Expected
- Log shows maxDimensionPx: 2000


### Actual
- Log shows maxDimensionPx: 1200 regardless of config


## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: Unit tests pass with config set to 2000, default {} when no config
- Edge cases checked: No config provided uses defaults
- What you did not verify: Live browser screenshot with actual image

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes)
- Config/env changes? (No)
- Migration needed? (No)

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert commit
- Files/config to restore: N/A
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: None expected

## Risks and Mitigations

- None
